theorems.formal_statement: forall {cacheSize current older newer : Nat}, kvCacheWindowContains cacheSize current older -> kvCacheWindowContains cacheSize current newer -> older < newer -> kvCacheSlot cacheSize older != kvCacheSlot cacheSize newer
theorems.informal_statement: Any two ordered tokens retained in the same KV-cache window occupy distinct ring-buffer slots.

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 2: Coil Attention and Memory

theorems.formal_statement: forall {cacheSize current left right : Nat}, kvCacheWindowContains cacheSize current left -> kvCacheWindowContains cacheSize current right -> left != right -> kvCacheSlot cacheSize left != kvCacheSlot cacheSize right
theorems.informal_statement: Any two distinct tokens retained in the same KV-cache window occupy distinct ring-buffer slots, without requiring the caller to order them first.

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 2: Coil Attention and Memory

theorems.formal_statement: forall {cacheSize current : Nat} {tokens : List Nat}, (forall token, token in tokens -> kvCacheWindowContains cacheSize current token) -> List.Pairwise (fun left right => left != right) tokens -> List.Pairwise (fun left right => kvCacheSlot cacheSize left != kvCacheSlot cacheSize right) tokens
theorems.informal_statement: A pairwise-distinct batch of tokens retained in the same KV-cache window maps to pairwise-distinct ring-buffer slots.

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 2: Coil Attention and Memory

theorems.formal_statement: forall {cacheSize current : Nat} {tokens : List Nat}, (forall token, token in tokens -> kvCacheWindowContains cacheSize current token) -> tokens.Nodup -> (tokens.map (kvCacheSlot cacheSize)).Nodup
theorems.informal_statement: A duplicate-free retained-token batch has duplicate-free KV-cache ring-buffer slots.

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 2: Coil Attention and Memory

theorems.formal_statement: forall {cacheSize current token : Nat}, Circle.Applications.kvCacheWindowContains cacheSize current token <-> token <= current and current < token + cacheSize
theorems.informal_statement: A token is retained in a KV-cache window exactly when it is not in the future and its next same-slot overwrite occurs after the current token.

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 2: Coil Attention and Memory

theorems.formal_statement: forall {cacheSize current token : Nat}, token <= current -> (not (Circle.Applications.kvCacheWindowContains cacheSize current token) <-> token + cacheSize <= current)
theorems.informal_statement: For a non-future token, not being retained in the KV-cache window is equivalent to its next same-slot overwrite being at or before the current token.

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 2: Coil Attention and Memory

theorems.formal_statement: forall cacheSize current : Nat, kvCacheLiveWindowStart cacheSize current + kvCacheLiveWindowLength cacheSize current = current + 1
theorems.informal_statement: The generated KV-cache live-window start plus live-window length ends exactly one token past the current token.

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 2: Coil Attention and Memory

theorems.formal_statement: forall {cacheSize current token : Nat}, Circle.Applications.kvCacheWindowContains cacheSize current token <-> token in Circle.Applications.kvCacheLiveWindowTokens cacheSize current
theorems.informal_statement: A token is retained in the KV-cache window exactly when it appears in the generated live-token list for that current position.

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 2: Coil Attention and Memory

theorems.formal_statement: forall cacheSize current : Nat, (Circle.Applications.kvCacheLiveWindowTokens cacheSize current).map (Circle.Applications.kvCacheSlot cacheSize) is duplicate-free
theorems.informal_statement: The generated live KV-cache window maps to duplicate-free ring-buffer slots.

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 2: Coil Attention and Memory

theorems.formal_statement: forall {cacheSize current : Nat}, 0 < cacheSize -> cacheSize <= current + 1 -> (Circle.Applications.kvCacheLiveWindowTokens cacheSize current).map (Circle.Applications.kvCacheSlot cacheSize) is duplicate-free and has length cacheSize and every emitted slot is less than cacheSize
theorems.informal_statement: Once a KV-cache live window is full, its generated slot list is duplicate-free, has exactly one entry per declared cache slot, and every emitted slot lies inside the cache range.

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 2: Coil Attention and Memory

theorems.formal_statement: forall {cacheSize current token overwrite : Nat}, Circle.Applications.kvCacheWindowContains cacheSize current token -> token < overwrite -> overwrite <= current -> Circle.Applications.kvCacheSlot cacheSize token != Circle.Applications.kvCacheSlot cacheSize overwrite
theorems.informal_statement: If a token is retained in the KV-cache window, no strictly later token position up to the current read point writes the same ring-buffer slot.

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 2: Coil Attention and Memory

theorems.formal_statement: forall {cacheSize current token : Nat}, 0 < cacheSize -> token <= current -> not (Circle.Applications.kvCacheWindowContains cacheSize current token) -> token < token + cacheSize and token + cacheSize <= current and Circle.Applications.kvCacheSlot cacheSize token = Circle.Applications.kvCacheSlot cacheSize (token + cacheSize)
theorems.informal_statement: For a non-future stale KV-cache token and positive cache size, token + cacheSize is a strictly later same-slot overwrite at or before the current read point.

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 2: Coil Attention and Memory

theorems.formal_statement: forall {cacheSize current token : Nat}, 0 < cacheSize -> token <= current -> (Circle.Applications.kvCacheWindowContains cacheSize current token <-> forall overwrite, token < overwrite -> overwrite <= current -> Circle.Applications.kvCacheSlot cacheSize token != Circle.Applications.kvCacheSlot cacheSize overwrite)
theorems.informal_statement: For a non-future token and positive cache size, KV-cache retention is equivalent to the absence of any later same-slot write up to the current read point.

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 2: Coil Attention and Memory

theorems.formal_statement: forall {cacheSize current : Nat} {tokens : List Nat}, 0 < cacheSize -> (forall token, token in tokens -> token <= current) -> ((forall token, token in tokens -> Circle.Applications.kvCacheWindowContains cacheSize current token) <-> forall token, token in tokens -> forall overwrite, token < overwrite -> overwrite <= current -> Circle.Applications.kvCacheSlot cacheSize token != Circle.Applications.kvCacheSlot cacheSize overwrite)
theorems.informal_statement: For a batch of non-future KV-cache tokens and positive cache size, all requested tokens are retained if and only if every requested token has no later same-slot writer up to the current read point.

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 2: Coil Attention and Memory

theorems.formal_statement: forall {cacheSize current : Nat} {tokens : List Nat}, 0 < cacheSize -> (forall token, token in tokens -> token <= current) -> (forall token, token in tokens -> forall overwrite, token < overwrite -> overwrite <= current -> Circle.Applications.kvCacheSlot cacheSize token != Circle.Applications.kvCacheSlot cacheSize overwrite) -> tokens.Nodup -> (tokens.map (Circle.Applications.kvCacheSlot cacheSize)).Nodup
theorems.informal_statement: A duplicate-free non-future KV-cache read batch whose tokens each have no later same-slot writer up to the current read point maps to duplicate-free ring-buffer slots.

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 2: Coil Attention and Memory

theorems.formal_statement: forall {cacheSize current : Nat}, length((Circle.Applications.kvCacheLiveWindowTokens cacheSize current).map (Circle.Applications.kvCacheSlot cacheSize)) = cacheSize <-> cacheSize <= current + 1
theorems.informal_statement: The generated KV-cache live-window slot map has exactly cacheSize entries if and only if the seen prefix is at least one full cache window.

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 2: Coil Attention and Memory

theorems.formal_statement: forall {cacheSize current : Nat}, 0 < cacheSize -> (the generated live-window slot map is duplicate-free and has length cacheSize and every emitted slot is less than cacheSize) <-> cacheSize <= current + 1
theorems.informal_statement: For a positive KV-cache size, the generated live-window slot map satisfies the full coverage contract if and only if the seen prefix is at least one full cache window.

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 2: Coil Attention and Memory

theorems.formal_statement: forall {cacheSize current slot : Nat}, 0 < cacheSize -> cacheSize <= current + 1 -> (slot in (Circle.Applications.kvCacheLiveWindowTokens cacheSize current).map (Circle.Applications.kvCacheSlot cacheSize) <-> slot < cacheSize)
theorems.informal_statement: For a positive KV-cache size and a full generated live window, a slot appears in the generated live-window slot map exactly when it is less than the cache size.

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 2: Coil Attention and Memory

theorems.formal_statement: forall {cacheSize current : Nat}, Circle.Applications.kvCacheLiveWindowTokens_slotRangeCovered cacheSize current <-> cacheSize <= current + 1
theorems.informal_statement: The generated KV-cache live-window slot map covers every declared cache slot exactly when the live window is full.

theorems.formal_statement: forall {cacheSize current : Nat} {tokens : List Nat}, 0 < cacheSize -> (Circle.Applications.kvCacheAdapterRequestTracePass cacheSize current tokens <-> (forall token, token in tokens -> token <= current) and tokens.Nodup and forall token, token in tokens -> Circle.Applications.kvCacheNoSameSlotOverwriteTrace cacheSize current token)

theorems.informal_statement: At the first loop step in a token-level recurrence schedule, every token in the declared range is active.

theorems.formal_statement: forall {loopPeriod step : Nat}, 0 < loopPeriod -> loopPeriod < step -> forall tokenCount : Nat, Circle.Applications.activeTokensAtStep loopPeriod tokenCount step = []

theorems.formal_statement: forall {loopPeriod token early late : Nat}, early <= late -> Circle.Applications.tokenActiveAtStep loopPeriod token late -> Circle.Applications.tokenActiveAtStep loopPeriod token early

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 2: Coil Attention and Memory

theorems.formal_statement: forall {loopPeriod tokenCount early late token : Nat}, early <= late -> token in Circle.Applications.activeTokensAtStep loopPeriod tokenCount late -> token in Circle.Applications.activeTokensAtStep loopPeriod tokenCount early
theorems.informal_statement: Later active-token lists are subsets of earlier active-token lists in a token-level recurrence schedule.

theorems.formal_statement: forall loopPeriod tokenCount step : Nat, (Circle.Applications.activeTokensAtStep loopPeriod tokenCount step).length <= tokenCount

theorems.formal_statement: forall {turnRatio margin : Real} {requestedContext certifiedContext : Nat}, requestedContext <= certifiedContext -> Circle.Applications.ropeTurnRatioFiniteMargin turnRatio margin certifiedContext -> Circle.Applications.ropeTurnRatioFiniteMargin turnRatio margin requestedContext
theorems.informal_statement: A finite-context turn-ratio margin certified for a larger context automatically applies to any smaller requested context.

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 4: Proof-Carrying RoPE Position Distinguishability

theorems.formal_statement: forall {frequencies : List Real} {frequency fullTurn margin tolerance : Real} {requestedContext certifiedContext left right : Nat}, requestedContext <= certifiedContext -> frequency in frequencies -> left < right -> right < requestedContext -> 0 <= frequency -> 0 < fullTurn -> Circle.Applications.ropeTurnRatioFiniteMargin (frequency / fullTurn) margin certifiedContext -> tolerance < fullTurn * margin -> not Circle.Applications.ropeRealPhaseBankNearTurn frequencies fullTurn tolerance left right
theorems.informal_statement: If one channel has a finite-context turn-ratio margin certified for a larger context, then every smaller requested context inherits the bank-level no-near-turn consequence.

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 4: Proof-Carrying RoPE Position Distinguishability

theorems.formal_statement: forall {periods : List Nat} {context left right : Nat}, context <= Circle.Applications.ropePeriodBankLCM periods -> left < right -> right < context -> not Circle.Applications.ropePhaseBankCollision periods left right
theorems.informal_statement: If the period-bank LCM reaches the inspected context, no unequal ordered in-context pair can collide in every declared integer-period RoPE channel.

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 4: Proof-Carrying RoPE Position Distinguishability

theorems.formal_statement: forall {turnRatio smallMargin largeMargin : Real} {context : Nat}, smallMargin <= largeMargin -> Circle.Applications.ropeTurnRatioFiniteMargin turnRatio largeMargin context -> Circle.Applications.ropeTurnRatioFiniteMargin turnRatio smallMargin context
theorems.informal_statement: A finite-context turn-ratio margin certified for a larger lower bound automatically proves any smaller advertised margin over the same context.

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 4: Proof-Carrying RoPE Position Distinguishability

theorems.formal_statement: forall {periods : List Nat} {context start : Nat}, start < Circle.Applications.ropeCollisionPairCountAtGap context (Circle.Applications.ropePeriodBankLCM periods) -> Circle.Applications.ropePhaseBankCollision periods start (start + Circle.Applications.ropePeriodBankLCM periods) and start + Circle.Applications.ropePeriodBankLCM periods < context
theorems.informal_statement: Every in-context start paired with the period-bank LCM gap gives an all-channel collision in the declared integer-period RoPE bank.

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 4: Proof-Carrying RoPE Position Distinguishability

theorems.formal_statement: forall {periods : List Nat} {context : Nat}, 0 < Circle.Applications.ropePeriodBankLCM periods -> Circle.Applications.ropePeriodBankLCM periods < context -> exists left right, left < right and right < context and Circle.Applications.ropePhaseBankCollision periods left right
theorems.informal_statement: If the period-bank LCM is positive and below the inspected context, the declared integer-period RoPE bank has an explicit unequal all-channel collision witness inside the context.

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 4: Proof-Carrying RoPE Position Distinguishability

theorems.formal_statement: forall {frequencies : List Real} {frequency fullTurn requestedMargin certifiedMargin tolerance : Real} {requestedContext certifiedContext left right : Nat}, requestedContext <= certifiedContext -> requestedMargin <= certifiedMargin -> frequency in frequencies -> left < right -> right < requestedContext -> 0 <= frequency -> 0 < fullTurn -> Circle.Applications.ropeTurnRatioFiniteMargin (frequency / fullTurn) certifiedMargin certifiedContext -> tolerance < fullTurn * requestedMargin -> not Circle.Applications.ropeRealPhaseBankNearTurn frequencies fullTurn tolerance left right
theorems.informal_statement: If one channel has a finite-context turn-ratio margin certified for a larger context and a larger lower bound, then every smaller requested context and smaller advertised margin inherits the bank-level no-near-turn consequence.

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 4: Proof-Carrying RoPE Position Distinguishability

theorems.formal_statement: forall {pref suffix : List Nat} {context left right : Nat}, context <= Circle.Applications.ropePeriodBankLCM pref -> left < right -> right < context -> not Circle.Applications.ropePhaseBankCollision (pref ++ suffix) left right
theorems.informal_statement: If a prefix subbank's LCM reaches the inspected context, then adding more integer-period RoPE channels cannot create an unequal all-channel collision.

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 4: Proof-Carrying RoPE Position Distinguishability

theorems.formal_statement: forall {subbank bank : List Nat} {context left right : Nat}, (forall period, period in subbank -> period in bank) -> context <= Circle.Applications.ropePeriodBankLCM subbank -> left < right -> right < context -> not Circle.Applications.ropePhaseBankCollision bank left right
theorems.informal_statement: If any selected subbank contained in a larger integer-period RoPE bank has LCM reaching the inspected context, then the larger bank cannot have an unequal all-channel collision.

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 4: Proof-Carrying RoPE Position Distinguishability

theorems.formal_statement: forall {turnRatio margin : Real} {turns : Int} {context : Nat}, turnRatio = (turns : Real) -> 1 < context -> (Circle.Applications.ropeTurnRatioFiniteMargin turnRatio margin context <-> margin <= 0)
theorems.informal_statement: If a real RoPE turn ratio is an integer number of full turns per position and the inspected context contains the unit gap, then its finite-context turn-ratio margin is equivalent to a nonpositive margin; such a channel cannot certify positive real-phase separation.

used_by_papers.title: Circle Calculus AI 4: Proof-Carrying RoPE Position Distinguishability

theorems.formal_statement: forall {turnRatio margin : Real} {context : Nat}, Circle.Applications.ropeTurnRatioFiniteMargin turnRatio margin context <-> forall gap : Nat, gap in List.range context -> 0 < gap -> forall turns : Int, margin <= Circle.Applications.ropeTurnRatioError turnRatio gap turns
theorems.informal_statement: A finite-context turn-ratio margin is equivalent to checking the same error lower bound on every positive gap contained in List.range context, giving the real RoPE margin program a theorem-backed finite gap-enumeration bridge.

theorems.formal_statement: forall {period left right : Nat}, left <= right -> Circle.Applications.phaseChannelCollision period left right <-> period | right - left

theorems.name: Phase-channel collision iff gap divisibility
theorems.proof_notes: Lean theorem is compiled in Circle/Applications/PositionPhase.lean from Nat.modEq_iff_dvd'.

theorems.formal_statement: forall {period left right : Nat}, left <= right -> Circle.Applications.phaseChannelDistinguishable period left right <-> not (period | right - left)
theorems.informal_statement: For ordered positions, one declared finite phase channel distinguishes them exactly when its period does not divide the gap.
theorems.name: Phase-channel distinguishability iff gap nondivisibility
theorems.proof_notes: Lean theorem is compiled in Circle/Applications/PositionPhase.lean by negating the single-channel collision criterion.

theorems.formal_statement: forall {period context left right : Nat}, context <= period -> left < context -> right < context -> Circle.Applications.phaseChannelCollision period left right <-> left = right
theorems.informal_statement: If the inspected context fits inside one declared period, then two in-context positions collide in that channel exactly when they are equal.
theorems.name: Phase-channel context injectivity
theorems.proof_notes: Lean theorem is compiled in Circle/Applications/PositionPhase.lean by reducing both residues with Nat.mod_eq_of_lt.

theorems.formal_statement: forall {periods : List Nat} {left right : Nat}, left <= right -> Circle.Applications.phaseBankCollision periods left right <-> forall period, period in periods -> period | right - left
theorems.informal_statement: For ordered positions, a finite phase bank collides exactly when every declared period divides the position gap.
theorems.name: Phase-bank collision iff all declared periods divide gap
theorems.proof_notes: Lean theorem is compiled in Circle/Applications/PositionPhase.lean by applying the single-channel divisibility criterion pointwise over the bank.

theorems.formal_statement: forall {periods : List Nat} {left right : Nat}, left <= right -> Circle.Applications.phaseBankDistinguishable periods left right <-> exists period, period in periods and not (period | right - left)
theorems.informal_statement: For ordered positions, a finite phase bank distinguishes them exactly when some declared period does not divide the position gap.
theorems.name: Phase-bank distinguishability iff some period does not divide gap
theorems.proof_notes: Lean theorem is compiled in Circle/Applications/PositionPhase.lean by existentially packaging a single-channel noncollision witness.

theorems.formal_statement: forall {periods : List Nat} {period context left right : Nat}, period in periods -> context <= period -> left < context -> right < context -> left != right -> Circle.Applications.phaseBankDistinguishable periods left right
theorems.informal_statement: If a phase bank contains a declared period at least as large as the inspected context, then every unequal pair inside the context is distinguished.
theorems.name: Phase-bank period at context distinguishes unequal in-context positions
theorems.proof_notes: Lean theorem is compiled in Circle/Applications/PositionPhase.lean from the single-channel context injectivity criterion.

theorems.formal_statement: forall {periods extended : List Nat} {left right : Nat}, (forall period, period in periods -> period in extended) -> Circle.Applications.phaseBankCollision extended left right -> Circle.Applications.phaseBankCollision periods left right
theorems.informal_statement: If a larger phase bank collides, then any declared subbank also collides.
theorems.name: Phase-bank collision restricts to subbanks
theorems.proof_notes: Lean theorem is compiled in Circle/Applications/PositionPhase.lean by applying the larger-bank collision hypothesis through the subset witness.

theorems.formal_statement: forall {periods extended : List Nat} {left right : Nat}, (forall period, period in periods -> period in extended) -> Circle.Applications.phaseBankDistinguishable periods left right -> Circle.Applications.phaseBankDistinguishable extended left right
theorems.informal_statement: A distinguishing channel in a subbank remains a distinguishing channel after adding more declared periods.
theorems.name: Phase-bank distinguishability is monotone under adding channels
theorems.proof_notes: Lean theorem is compiled in Circle/Applications/PositionPhase.lean by reusing the existing witness channel in the extended bank.

theorems.formal_statement: forall {xPeriods yPeriods : List Nat} {leftX rightX leftY rightY : Nat}, Circle.Applications.phaseGrid2DCollision xPeriods yPeriods leftX rightX leftY rightY <-> Circle.Applications.phaseBankCollision xPeriods leftX rightX and Circle.Applications.phaseBankCollision yPeriods leftY rightY

theorems.formal_statement: forall {scale period : Nat}, 0 < scale -> 0 < period -> 0 < Circle.Applications.scaledPhasePeriod scale period
